description_long: This is a rectangular block of stone with a roughly hewn, natural appearance and a muted palette of greenish-gray, beige, and brown hues. The surfaces are mostly flat but uneven, with visible striations and subtle color variations that suggest mineral veining. One end of the stone features a slightly rougher, chipped texture, while the opposite end has a smoother, more uniform face with a circular label or marking. The stone's edges are irregular and not sharply defined, giving it a somewhat weathered look. The overall form is solid and dense, with no visible cavities or openings. Its size and shape make it suitable as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, such as a park or sidewalk.
description: A rectangular, greenish-gray stone block with rough, uneven surfaces and subtle mineral veining. It has a weathered appearance and a solid, dense form.
description_view_0: This view shows the stone's broad, flat face with a gentle gradient of greenish-gray and beige tones, highlighting its natural striations.
description_view_1: Here, the elongated side of the stone is visible, displaying more pronounced color variation and a slightly rougher texture along the length.
description_view_2: This angle reveals the stone's base and a circular label or marking on one end, with the roughest and most irregular surface visible.
description_view_3: The end face with the label is prominent, showing a smoother, lighter surface and the block's overall rectangular geometry.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a barrier along pathways
attribute_pattern: Subtle mineral veining and color mottling with irregular, natural striations; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Slightly weathered with some rough, chipped edges and a generally matte, unpolished surface.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts moderately with paved surfaces but can blend with natural landscaping elements such as grass or gravel.
